scope: Large board-impact batch covering 50 next-priority unprojected players, skipping rows without confirmed current teams and prioritizing returners/transfers with official roster or signing evidence.
method: Skip rows already present in earlier research batches.
Skip transfer rows whose current-team field is blank in the projection export.
Prioritize remaining unprojected rows by historical rating, production anchor, projected lineup relevance, and official roster confirmation.
Use official roster/signing pages first, with reputable local/team coverage only where official context is thin.
Keep every row pending admin approval so the batch can be reviewed and applied in one pass.

formulas.projectedBbprWithHistory: historicalRating/proven production blended with projection score, then calibrated by player type

teamContextRubric.projectedStarter: Yes requires official/staff role language, prior starter evidence, or a clear projected lineup path; Uncertain means major-minute profile without a locked starting signal; No means bench/depth role.
teamContextRubric.projectedRole: 92+ for clear high-usage starters, 86-91 for likely starters or closing-lineup players, 78-85 for rotation players with role caps.
teamContextRubric.opportunityChange: Rewards open usage, returning hierarchy, and transfer fit; penalizes crowded high-major rotations or uncertain eligibility/role translation.
teamContextRubric.offensiveBurden: Primary scorers/creators get 86+; defensive specialists, rim-running bigs, and low-usage connectors stay lower.
teamContextRubric.roleEvidence: Every row includes why starter credit was assigned or capped.

players.researchSummary: Fuchs brings proven WCC frontcourt production to a Clemson roster with available frontcourt minutes. He gets major-rotation/possible-starter credit, but Clemson's post depth and moderate NBA signal keep him below a top-option transfer tier.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Clemson roster lists him as a senior forward after San Francisco, and transfer coverage frames him as a feasible starting post option.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely frontcourt piece, but scoring burden should be shared with Clemson guards and other bigs.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Frontcourt need is real, though Faulkner, Steinour, Welling health, and other forwards keep the role short of locked-star status.

evidence.notes: Official roster lists Fuchs on Clemson's 2026-27 team.
Transfer report cites WCC Newcomer of the Year production and Clemson frontcourt need.

players.researchSummary: Dual has defensive/playmaking value and a former top-50 recruit signal, but Syracuse has multiple new guards/wings and his McNeese scoring profile was secondary. He should be treated as a high-rotation guard with starter upside rather than a primary scorer.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Syracuse announced Dual after a McNeese season with 17 starts, strong assists, and all-defensive recognition.
teamContext.roleCap: Defensive connector and secondary creator, not a clear top offensive option.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Doty, Goodrick, Anthony, White, and other Syracuse pieces keep usage distributed.

evidence.notes: Official signing page confirms Dual's transfer and McNeese production.

players.researchSummary: Pippen is a high-major lead-guard transfer after starting at Cal and earning honorable mention All-ACC. Ohio State has guard competition, but his two-way production and assist/steal profile justify starter-level scoring.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Ohio State announced him as an experienced guard addition after 32 Cal starts.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starter or closing guard, with usage shared alongside Mobley and other Buckeye guards.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Backcourt depth keeps him just below a no-doubt primary-creator score.

evidence.notes: Official release cites 14.2 PPG, 4.6 APG, steals, and All-ACC honorable mention at Cal.

players.researchSummary: Clark's Seton Hall two-way guard profile gives Ole Miss a credible starting guard candidate. He is bumped for Big East creation/defense but capped because the SEC role and offensive hierarchy are not fully settled.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Transfer coverage notes his Ole Miss commitment after a strong Seton Hall season.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starter/closing guard, but not a guaranteed primary scorer.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Ole Miss transfer depth and SEC translation keep confidence moderate.

evidence.notes: Reports Clark's move to Ole Miss and cites All-BIG EAST/defensive recognition.

players.researchSummary: Richmond is a versatile Arkansas returner with All-Defensive/energy value and starting experience. His offensive burden is capped, but the all-around role and NBA tools deserve a clear projection.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Arkansas bio lists a junior wing who started 19 games and played every game last season.
teamContext.roleCap: Defensive/transition glue role rather than alpha scoring.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Small Arkansas roster snapshot raises minutes, while scoring load still depends on additions.

evidence.notes: Official bio cites All-Defensive honors, games played, starts, and versatility.

players.researchSummary: Catchings returns after starting all 32 games and flashing real SEC scoring/shooting growth. He earns starter-level projection with a development bump, while Georgia's broader roster keeps him out of the elite-usage range.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Georgia bio confirms 32 starts and late-season SEC production.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely starting forward/scorer, but shared Bulldog creation caps top-option burden.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Georgia has multiple guards/transfers, though Catchings owns one of the cleanest returning forward paths.

evidence.notes: Official bio lists 2025-26 starts, scoring, rebounding, and SEC shooting surge.

players.researchSummary: Byrd's Mountain West Defensive Player of the Year profile should travel to Providence and gives him a strong starting/closing case. Offensive burden stays capped because his value is defense, size, and winning experience more than shot dominance.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Providence announced him as an experienced guard addition with four years of San Diego State winning context.
teamContext.roleCap: High-minute defender and connector, not a primary offensive option.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Friars added several guards, but Byrd's defensive identity should keep him in the rotation core.

evidence.notes: Official release confirms transfer and Mountain West Defensive Player of the Year context.

players.researchSummary: Yigitoglu gives Indiana major size and a frontcourt transfer centerpiece, but Aiden Sherrell and other portal pieces make exact usage uncertain. Score him as a big rotation/starter candidate with defensive/tools value.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Indiana roster lists him as a 7-2 junior center from SMU, and offseason coverage highlights the portal class.
teamContext.roleCap: Starting center path is plausible, but shared frontcourt minutes cap role certainty.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Sherrell, Winker, and younger bigs keep the rotation unsettled.

evidence.notes: Official roster confirms Yigitoglu on Indiana's 2026-27 team.
Roster-rebuild article lists Yigitoglu among Indiana's transfer additions.

players.researchSummary: Garrett projects as a featured High Point frontcourt scorer after starting every game at Oakland and posting efficient scoring/rebounding. National upside is modest, but role confidence is high.

teamContext.starterEvidence: High Point signing release lists all-rotation production and every-game starter context.
teamContext.roleCap: Likely featured Big South piece, but NBA/tools signal keeps him outside national-star scoring.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Transfer class adds options, yet Garrett's Oakland production should command minutes.

evidence.notes: Official signing page cites 13.7 PPG, 7.1 RPG, and 28 starts at Oakland.

players.researchSummary: Washington reunites with Richard Pitino as a proven defensive guard who started at Miami. He gets likely-starter credit but is capped below lead-guard scoring because Xavier has several transfer creators.

teamContext.starterEvidence: Xavier signing page cites 19 Miami starts and Pitino familiarity.
teamContext.roleCap: Winning guard/defender with secondary scoring burden.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Dominguez, Nwoko, Westry, and other additions distribute usage.

evidence.notes: Official release confirms Washington's signing and Miami/New Mexico production.

players.researchSummary: Boozer has a strong development profile and Duke guard size, but his role depends heavily on Duke's full backcourt mix. This gives him sixth-starter upside without assuming primary usage.

teamContext.roleCap: Rotation guard with breakout path, but not enough evidence for locked-starter treatment.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Foster, Evans, Harris, and other Duke wings/guards keep touches competitive.

players.researchSummary: Pettiford needs a major lift from the raw historical anchor: Auburn's own offseason language frames the backcourt as his and Kevin Overton's team, and his sophomore production was clear lead-guard material.

teamContext.roleCap: Primary/secondary creator with top-100 guard case.
teamContext.positionCompetition: Overton shares usage, but both should anchor Auburn's guard hierarchy.

Staff comments describe Pettiford and Overton as the veteran backcourt core.

teamContext.roleCap: Likely starting lead guard with strong creation burden.
